data_schema: key(str id, keep verbatim for submission); fare_amount(float target, USD, >0); pickup_datetime(str 'YYYY-MM-DD HH:MM:SS UTC'); pickup_longitude/pickup_latitude/dropoff_longitude/dropoff_latitude(float degrees); passenger_count(int). Target column fare_amount is present in train.csv only. test.csv has the 7 non-target columns.
extra_notes: Kaggle NYC Taxi Fare. Metric = RMSE on fare_amount (LOWER is better). train.csv=900k rows (RAM is ample, no need to subsample). Feature engineering that matters: parse pickup_datetime into year/month/day/hour/weekday; compute haversine distance between pickup/dropoff; abs longitude/latitude diffs; drop or clip physically-impossible rows (fare<=0, coords outside NYC bbox lon[-75,-72] lat[40,42], passenger_count 0 or >8) in TRAIN ONLY (never drop test rows). Use gradient boosting (lightgbm/xgboost/catboost available). Report CV_SCORE as local K-fold RMSE (lower better). submission.csv MUST have exactly columns key,fare_amount with all 100000 test keys in original order. metrics.json must contain {"cv_score": <rmse float>, "metric": "rmse"}.
